title: Funded Trading Plus Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Funded Trading Plus Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Funded Trading Plus (4.4★, $119/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Funded Trading Plus — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Funded Trading Plus?

Funded Trading Plus is a UK-based proprietary trading firm founded in 2013 that provides traders with access to funded trading accounts without requiring them to risk their own capital. Operating from Manchester under FTP London Ltd., the company has distributed over $19.5 million to more than 60,000 traders and maintains a 4.4/5 rating with an impressive 4.7/5 Trustpilot score. The platform offers three entry-level plans starting at $119 per month, with an instant funding option at $299 per month that bypasses the evaluation phase entirely. Funded Trading Plus is ideal for forex traders, particularly those in the UK and Europe, who want immediate access to funded capital, flexibility in platform choice, or a clear scaling path toward larger accounts.

How to Create Your Funded Trading Plus Account

Creating a Funded Trading Plus account takes approximately 10-15 minutes and requires minimal documentation. Follow these steps to get started:

  • Visit the registration page: Go to the Funded Trading Plus website and click "Sign Up" or "Get Started." You'll be directed to a registration form.
  • Enter basic information: Provide your email address, full name, country of residence, and create a secure password. Funded Trading Plus accepts traders globally, but the platform is particularly optimized for UK and European participants.
  • Verify your email: Check your inbox for a verification email and click the confirmation link. This typically arrives within minutes.
  • Complete your profile: Fill in additional details including trading experience level, preferred markets (forex, indices, commodities, or crypto), and your trading strategy. This information helps Funded Trading Plus match you with the appropriate account type.
  • Identity verification: You'll need to upload a government-issued ID (passport, driving license, or national ID card) and proof of address (utility bill or bank statement dated within the last 3 months). This verification process usually completes within 24 hours.
  • Choose your account type: Select between the 2-Step Classic ($119/mo), 1-Step Express ($119/mo), or Instant Funding ($299/mo) option based on your preference for evaluation time versus immediate funding.
  • Complete payment: Funded Trading Plus accepts credit cards and bank transfers. Once payment is processed, your account becomes active immediately, and you can begin trading if you selected Instant Funding, or start the evaluation if you chose a Classic or Express plan.

Setting Up Funded Trading Plus for the First Time

After account creation, the first login presents you with a clean dashboard that serves as your command center. The main dashboard displays your account balance, equity, profit/loss percentage, and open positions in real-time. Take time to familiarize yourself with the top navigation menu, which typically includes sections for Platforms, Risk Rules, Performance Analytics, and Support.

Connect your trading platform: Funded Trading Plus supports five platforms—MetaTrader 4, MetaTrader 5, cTrader, DXTrade, and Ninja Trader—giving you flexibility based on your preferred charting and execution tools. Choose your platform in the Platforms section of your dashboard. The system will provide trading credentials (server address, username, and password) that you'll input directly into your chosen platform's login screen. This connection establishes the live link between your Funded Trading Plus account and your trading terminal within 2-5 minutes.

Configure risk management settings: Before placing your first trade, navigate to Risk Rules or similar section in your dashboard. This is where Funded Trading Plus enforces its key risk protections. You'll see parameters like maximum daily loss limits and maximum trade size, which vary by account type. Understanding these boundaries prevents accidental violations that could halt your trading. Most traders take 5-10 minutes to review and understand these rules fully.

Set up alerts and notifications: Enable email or in-app notifications for margin calls, profit targets reached, or daily loss limit warnings. This keeps you informed in real-time if your account approaches risk thresholds, which is especially valuable if you're away from your computer.

Review performance analytics: The built-in performance analytics dashboard shows detailed metrics on win rate, profit factor, average trade size, and more. Familiarize yourself with how to access these reports—they become essential for refining your strategy during evaluation phases or while scaling your account.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Automated Trading Support - Funded Trading Plus enables algorithmic and EA-based trading across supported platforms, allowing you to deploy automated strategies 24/5 on forex markets. Unlike some competitors that restrict bot trading, Funded Trading Plus explicitly permits automation, making it ideal for traders developing systematic strategies. Your robots execute using the same risk rules and performance metrics as manual trading.

2. Performance Analytics - The built-in analytics engine tracks 15+ trading metrics including win rate, profit factor, consecutive wins/losses, average trade duration, and risk-reward ratio. These detailed reports help you identify edge in your strategy and are provided in real-time as you trade. During evaluation phases, these analytics determine whether you advance to funded status.

3. Multi-Platform Access - Supporting MetaTrader 4/5, cTrader, DXTrade, and Ninja Trader, Funded Trading Plus gives you rare flexibility in choosing your trading interface. If you're a cTrader user seeking prop trading, this is one of the few firms offering that combination. You can even run multiple platforms simultaneously against the same account, though you'll share the same risk limits across all connections.

4. Risk Management System - The automated risk management enforces daily loss limits, maximum trade size restrictions, and drawdown parameters specific to your account tier. These protections are built into the trading system—orders that violate risk rules are automatically rejected before execution, preventing account blowouts. The clarity and consistency of these rules is a major advantage for traders who want no surprises.

5. Scaling Path Up to $2.5 Million - As you prove profitability, Funded Trading Plus's growth program automatically scales your account. Traders starting at $10,000-$25,000 can progress through predetermined tiers reaching $2.5 million, with profit splits remaining at 80-100% depending on your tier. This scaling is transparent and rule-based rather than discretionary.

6. Profit Splits Scaling Up to 100% - Funded Trading Plus offers unusually high profit retention, starting at 70-80% and scaling to 100% as you maintain consistent profitability. This means once you hit higher tiers, you keep every dollar your strategy generates. Few competitors offer true 100% splits at any level.

7. Education and Community Resources - The platform provides webinars, trading courses, and community forums populated with both successful funded traders and Funded Trading Plus staff. These resources, combined with 24/7 customer support via email and chat, make it easier to troubleshoot issues or get guidance on rule compliance.

Funded Trading Plus Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Funded Trading Plus offers three distinct pricing models, each suited to different trader profiles and objectives:

2-Step Classic - $119/month - This is the traditional evaluation path. You pay $119 upfront and must pass a two-step evaluation (60 days + 30 days) meeting profit targets before receiving your funded account. Initial account size typically ranges from $10,000-$25,000. This plan appeals to disciplined traders with proven strategies who don't mind a waiting period and want to test their approach under realistic pressure. The 60-day evaluation ensures you've demonstrated consistency, which builds confidence for the prop firm to scale you later.

1-Step Express - $119/month - This accelerated version compresses the evaluation into a single 60-day phase instead of two sequential phases. You're still required to meet profit targets, but you reach funded status 30 days faster than Classic. The cost remains $119/month, making it an easy upgrade if you qualify. Choose Express if you have an already-validated trading system and want faster access to your funded account without paying more.

Instant Funding - $299/month - This premium option skips evaluation entirely. Upon payment, you receive immediate funded capital ($25,000 starting tier) and begin trading right away. You still adhere to risk rules, but there's no performance threshold to clear. This plan costs $180 more monthly than Classic ($299 vs. $119), but it's appropriate for experienced traders with strong track records who value immediate execution and don't want to navigate an evaluation phase. The $299 investment is recovered quickly if your strategy has positive expected value.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners new to prop trading should start with 2-Step Classic to build discipline and verify their edge under structured conditions. Intermediate traders with documented profitability should choose 1-Step Express to reduce time-to-funding. Advanced traders or those scaling from other prop firms should select Instant Funding to deploy capital immediately and focus on scaling rather than evaluation metrics.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Funded Trading Plus

  • Document your strategy before starting evaluation. Write down your entry/exit rules, position sizing formula, and maximum daily loss threshold before funding your account. This prevents emotional decision-making during high-pressure evaluation phases and ensures you trade consistently against the Funded Trading Plus performance metrics you'll be judged on.
  • Use cTrader if you scalp or trade volatility. While all five platforms are supported, cTrader's superior execution speed and Level II pricing visibility give scalpers and volatility traders an edge that MetaTrader doesn't match. If your strategy involves holding trades under one hour, cTrader is your optimal choice within Funded Trading Plus.
  • Exploit the 100% profit split tier aggressively. Unlike competitors capping splits at 80%, Funded Trading Plus eventually offers 100% retention. Structure your scaling strategy explicitly to reach that tier—it fundamentally changes your mathematics. A $50,000-account trader at 100% splits generates real income; at 80%, it's merely supplemental.
  • Track your performance metrics weekly against the analytics dashboard. Funded Trading Plus provides granular data on win rate, profit factor, and risk-reward ratio. Review these weekly to catch strategies degrading before it impacts your monthly drawdown or evaluation passing. Many traders only check performance monthly and miss mid-month deviations they could correct.
  • Set up multiple accounts if testing different strategies. Funded Trading Plus allows funded traders to run separate accounts for different systems simultaneously. Use a Classic account to evaluate a new strategy in parallel while your Instant Funding account trades your proven system—this allows strategy experimentation without risking your primary capital.
  • Join the community forums for rule clarification. Each prop firm interprets gray-area rules differently. The Funded Trading Plus forums are where traders discuss whether specific order types, rollover trades, or multi-instrument correlations are permitted. This prevents surprise account closure from rule misunderstandings.
  • Request account reviews from support before scaling. When approaching a scaling threshold, email support asking them to review your trading pattern for rule compliance. They'll flag any behaviors that might trigger review at the next tier, letting you adjust proactively rather than discovering issues after scaling.

Common Funded Trading Plus Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Platform connection fails on first login. After receiving trading credentials, some users can't connect MetaTrader or cTrader. Solution: Verify you're using the exact server address provided by Funded Trading Plus (not your broker's standard server). Clear your platform cache by closing it completely, deleting the broker folder in AppData/Local (Windows) or Library (Mac), then restarting the platform. If issues persist, contact support via the dashboard chat—they'll verify your credentials and occasionally reissue them.

Issue 2: Trades are rejected with "insufficient margin" despite having account balance. This occurs because Funded Trading Plus calculates margin differently than retail brokers, and its risk management rejects trades that exceed position sizing limits. Solution: Check your Risk Rules in the dashboard to see maximum position size and leverage limits. Your individual trade size must be smaller than these thresholds. Reduce position size or use tighter stops if you're hitting these limits frequently—this is intentional design to prevent overleveraging.

Issue 3: Profit splits payment is delayed beyond the stated timeline. Funded Trading Plus typically processes payouts monthly, but some traders report extended delays. Solution: Payouts process on a set schedule (usually mid-month). If your withdrawal is overdue by more than 5 business days, contact support with your account and withdrawal request dates. Most delays are administrative errors resolvable within 48 hours once escalated to the finance team.

Issue 4: Unable to pass evaluation due to missing profit targets. This is psychological, not technical, but common. Solution: Review your strategy's edge using the performance analytics—if win rate, profit factor, or risk-reward ratio appear solid, the evaluation miss may be variance or position sizing error rather than strategy failure. Many successful traders fail first attempts. Analyze what broke (discipline? position size? market conditions?) and attempt again. The $119 cost of retrying is trivial compared to the $25,000+ funded account you'll unlock.

Is Funded Trading Plus Worth It? Our Verdict

Funded Trading Plus deserves serious consideration from forex and indices traders, particularly those based in the UK or Europe seeking flexible platform choice and transparent scaling paths. The 4.4/5 rating, combined with $19.5M+ in actual payouts to 60,000+ traders, proves the firm operates legitimately and at scale. The Instant Funding option at $299/month is genuinely differentiated—most competitors force evaluation, making it uniquely valuable for traders who've already proven themselves elsewhere. However, the higher entry costs versus some competitors (notably FTMO starting at lower tiers) and the lack of a native mobile app are trade-offs. Compare directly with 5ER if you prioritize lower fees or TopStep if you're in the US and need domestic support. Choose Funded Trading Plus if you value multi-platform flexibility, fast scaling to $2.5M, and don't mind paying a premium for those advantages.
